page: Amazon Web Services c8gd.large is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 2 dedicated physical cores on the ARM64 architecture using the Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.8 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 4.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB memory-per-core ratio. It is equipped with 118 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-threaded capabilities for specific tasks, such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core and multi-threaded performance metrics, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core tests, fall into the weak category. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for large block read-only operations but weak performance for small block reads and large block writes. Passmark database operations and Geekbench text processing achieve average scores, while cryptography and LLM inference tasks show poor to weak performance.

The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage makes this instance class qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring low-latency temporary storage without the overhead of network-attached volumes. The dedicated core allocation ensures consistent compute performance, though the overall resource footprint is constrained by the 2 vCPU and 4.0 GB RAM limits. This server is suited for lightweight single-threaded processing, basic database operations, and local caching applications.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated ARM64 cores powered by the 2.8 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. The instance is configured with 4.0 GB of system memory and 118 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, operating on the Nitro hypervisor with a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show strong single-threaded performance in compression tasks, alongside average scores in text processing and database operations. However, multi-threaded workloads, memory write bandwidth, and cryptographic operations show weaker performance characteristics. This resource profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that benefit from dedicated ARM cores and fast local temporary storage, such as lightweight web serving, basic database tasks, and single-threaded data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.large is a compute-optimized ARM64 server with 2 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 118 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, suitable for lightweight database and compression tasks.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.large is an ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring 2 Graviton cores, 4.0 GB RAM, and 118 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores and integrated local NVMe SSD storage for efficient single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARM64 processor with 2 dedicated cores
4.0 GB system memory and 118 GB local NVMe SSD instance storage
Strong single-threaded performance in compression and decompression tasks
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth running on the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for lightweight database operations and single-threaded processing
